description: VibeIDE model behaviour quirks. Source of truth — see README.md section 'Model behaviour quirks'. PRs welcome. Schema: ./src/vs/workbench/contrib/vibeide/common/modelQuirks/modelQuirksTypes.ts.

rules.note: Legacy kimi-k2 (non-thinking, non-versioned) — opencode upstream uses T=0.6
Future kimi versions — defaults to 'new-style' preset
Base minimax-m2 — opencode upstream uses topK=20; m2.x variants prefer topK=40
Future minimax versions — defaults to higher topK

kimi via openCode aggregator — native FC unreliable (auto-downgrade fires repeatedly on tool-call errors). Force XML up front to skip warmup. Direct Moonshot API users get the regular kimi-* family rules below without XML forcing.

DeepSeek via openCode aggregator — native FC confuses tool names with cross-tool args (e.g. names a tool 'read_file' but sends search_for_files-shaped args). Force XML. Direct DeepSeek API works correctly with native FC — falls through to the unscoped deepseek rule below.

DeepSeek family (all providers) — every assistant message must carry a reasoning slot (HTTP 400 otherwise); reasoning text must be mirrored on top-level message field. Native FC is left enabled here for direct API users; the openCode-scoped rule above forces XML for that aggregator.

Qwen-family — native FC unreliable (hallucinates param names like 'path_pattern' instead of 'pattern'). Forcing XML mode routes the output through extractXMLToolsWrapper which handles the naked-tag <grep><pattern>...</pattern></grep> format the model naturally emits.
Gemini via aggregator (NOT native @ai-sdk/google path, which has its own settings)
